metadata.title:
WASHINGTON LAW STRENGTHENS


CONTENTION FOR POLICE MATRON

WOMAN'S CLUB BELIEVES IT
HAS FOUND A WAY TO
OVERCOME oPPo-
-. SITION.

pages.raw_text:
An act requiring the appointment
of police matrons in certain cities,
designating their duties and direct.
ing their compensation, and declar.
ing an emergency. Approved Feb.
20, 1898.


. 80 %>><<>><<<<>
. Section 3674-Police Ma q
= irons. There shall be annexed .
= to the police force Of each =
. city in this state having a .
. population Of not less than q
= 10000 inhabitants, one or .
. more police matrons, who, .
. subject to the control of the =
= chief Of police or other proper .
. officer, shall have the imme- .
= date care Of all females un- .
= der arrest and while detained q
= in the city prison, until they .
= are finally discharged there =

1t is on the strength of this Sec
ton OF the Washington state law,
found on page $50 of Pierce's code
of 1905. that the Woman's club of
Spokane is likely to make q fight
for the appointment of an official
matron or matrons at the city Jail.
Since the city council and Chief
of Police Sullivan defeated their
first attempts to have a few women
added to the police force to have
charge of the female prisoners,
members of the Woman's club have
been looking up the law and their
discovery of the above quoted Sec
ton of the Washington laws is the
result.


They contend that the words
There shall be annexed" is plain
enough for any one to understand
and that some woman officers
should be annexed to the depart
ment without delay.
